events.name: US CHIPS and Science Act of 2022
events.description: Landmark US$280B legislation establishing semiconductor manufacturing incentives, research investment, and workforce development programmes. Creates extraterritorial operating restrictions on funded entities, reshaping global semiconductor supply chain geography.
events.investment: US$280B authorized (US$52.7B CHIPS funding)

basel5.rationale: US$280B authorized (US$52.7B direct CHIPS funding) far exceeds US$500M threshold
Restructures global semiconductor supply chains; attracts TSMC, Samsung, Intel expansion
Creates alternative to Asia-concentrated manufacturing; only US programme at this scale
Multi-year implementation (FY2023-2027); cutting-edge process nodes
Affects manufacturing decisions by TSMC, Samsung, Intel globally; restricts China expansion

events.tierRationale: Tier 2: Between-Bloc Fragmentation (Buildout). The CHIPS and Science Act conditions its manufacturing incentives on Section 103 guardrails that prohibit funded entities from expanding capacity in countries of concern — a binding cross-bloc operating restriction. The programme's supporting documentation frames it explicitly around China: dependency-reduction language cites Taiwan and China risk, national-security justifications co-name China in the White House Fact Sheet, and supply-chain resilience is defined against Chinese concentration. The resulting fragmentation is structural — domestic buildout fenced off from the opposing bloc by statutory condition.

blocAnalysis.role: United States (primary); attracts TSMC, Samsung, Intel

events.name: China Gallium/Germanium Export Licensing
events.description: Export licensing controls on gallium, germanium, and related compounds affecting dominant shares of global supply. China imposed licensing controls requiring prior approval, technology disclosures, and end-user certificates per MOFCOM Announcement No. 23 of 2023, targeting critical inputs for semiconductor manufacturing.
events.investment: >$3B estimated supply impact

basel5.rationale: >90% global gallium; ~60% germanium — market concentration qualifies (US$3B+ est. trade impact)
Creates critical cross-border dependencies; restricts Western AI chip manufacturing inputs
Highly concentrated supply (90%+ gallium); few alternatives exist
Policy instrument, not infrastructure
Affects AI chip supply chains across multiple Western nations

events.tierRationale: Tier 2: Between-Bloc Fragmentation (Restriction — Impact-Demonstrated). MOFCOM Announcement No. 23 of 2023 names no country or bloc — the licensing regime is application-agnostic on its face. With China holding over 90% of global gallium and roughly 60% of germanium supply, the controls drew formal policy responses from multiple other-bloc governments. The cross-bloc fragmentation is therefore demonstrated through documented reaction rather than asserted through targeting language — the distinguishing feature of the Impact-Demonstrated route.

events.name: China Administrative Provisions on Algorithm Recommendation
events.investment: Regulatory Framework (Penalties RMB 10,000-100,000; 30+ algorithms filed August 2022)
events.description: World's first comprehensive binding regulatory framework for recommendation algorithms. Establishes mandatory algorithm filing system, transparency requirements, and user control mechanisms for AI-powered content curation. Regulates generation/synthesis, personalized recommendation, ranking/selection, retrieval/filtering, and dispatching/decision-making algorithm technologies. Creates foundational regulatory infrastructure that subsequent AI regulations (Deep Synthesis, Generative AI Interim Measures) operationalise via explicit statutory cross-references. Joint issuance by CAC, MIIT, MPS, and SAMR.

events.name: China Administrative Provisions on Deep Synthesis
events.investment: Regulatory Framework (1,919 deep synthesis service algorithms filed across 7 batches (as of August 2024); enforcement via Data Security Law/PIPL)
events.description: Comprehensive regulation targeting AI-generated synthetic media including deepfakes. Article 23 provides detailed legal definitions covering six AI technology categories: text generation, speech synthesis, audio generation, biometric editing (face-swap, voice cloning), image/video generation, and virtual scene generation. Establishes mandatory content labelling requirements, consent frameworks for biometric data, and algorithm filing procedures. Article 19 explicitly requires compliance with Algorithm Recommendation Provisions' filing system. Joint issuance by CAC, MIIT, and MPS.

events.investment: Fiscal conditionality (no direct financial allocation); phased exclusion: battery components from 2024, critical minerals from 2025; Section 30D credit value US$7,500/US$3,750 per vehicle; qualifying models reduced from 43 to 19; covered nations: PRC, Russia, DPRK, Iran.

events.name: UNGA AI Scientific Panel and Global Dialogue (A/RES/79/325)
events.location: New York, United States (UN General Assembly)
events.scope: International (193 UN Member States; adopted by consensus without a vote)
events.description: UNGA Resolution A/RES/79/325, adopted by consensus on 26 August 2025, establishes the Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence and the Global Dialogue on Artificial Intelligence Governance. The Scientific Panel is mandated to produce annual non-prescriptive assessment reports synthesising AI research. The resolution follows from the Global Digital Compact (A/RES/79/1) framework.
events.investment: Consensus adoption by 193 Member States; 123 co-sponsors; 40-member Scientific Panel (3-year terms); both mechanisms launched at 80th General Assembly session September 2025; Dialogue convened annually alternating Geneva and New York.

events.name: EU Critical Raw Materials Act
events.scope: European Union (27 Member States, extraterritorial reach)
events.description: Regulation (EU) 2024/1252 establishes a framework for secure and sustainable supply of critical raw materials across the European Union, designating 17 strategic and 34 critical raw materials including gallium, germanium, lithium, cobalt, rare earth elements, and graphite. The European Critical Raw Materials Board coordinates binding extraction, processing, and recycling benchmarks with a single-country concentration cap.
events.investment: €22.5 billion across 47 Strategic Projects (March 2025). At least 10% EU extraction, 40% processing, 25% recycling of annual consumption by 2030; 65% single-country cap.

events.name: Australia Critical Minerals Production Tax Incentive
events.description: The Critical Minerals Production Tax Incentive provides a 10% refundable tax offset for eligible costs of processing 31 designated critical minerals in Australia. events.name: Hygon DCU Series
events.description: Hygon Information Technology's DCU series comprises three generations of GPGPU-architecture AI accelerators in mass production for training and inference workloads, operating within a CUDA-compatible software ecosystem supporting deployment across Chinese AI infrastructure.
events.investment: 2024 revenue RMB 9.162B (+52.4% YoY); gross margin 63.72%; current generation FP16 49 TFLOPS

industry_tags.primary_evidence: GPGPU AI accelerator chip design and mass production — Priority Group 1 semiconductor keyword match

events.name: Kunlunxin P800
events.description: Kunlunxin's P800, a third-generation AI accelerator based on the proprietary XPU-P architecture, entered mass production in 2024. A Baidu subsidiary, Kunlunxin develops purpose-built AI training and inference processors for the Chinese domestic market.
events.investment: 2024 revenue ~RMB 2B (net loss ~RMB 200M); Baidu 57.67% shareholding; 69,000 units shipped — second among Chinese AI chip producers per IDC 2024

industry_tags.primary_evidence: AI accelerator chip design and mass production — Priority Group 1 semiconductor keyword match

events.name: Tianshu Zhixin GPU Series
events.description: Tianshu Zhixin (Iluvatar CoreX) develops dual-line GPGPU products: the Tian'ai series for AI training and the Zhikai series for inference, listing on HKEX in January 2026. The Tian'ai 100 was the first Chinese-designed 7nm cloud training GPU at time of its 2021 release.
events.investment: 2025 revenue RMB 1.034B (+91.6% YoY); gross margin 54.0%; 52,000+ GPUs deployed to 290+ customers as of mid-2025

industry_tags.primary_evidence: GPGPU AI accelerator design with training and inference product lines — Priority Group 1 semiconductor/GPU keyword match

events.name: South Korea AI Basic Act (Law No. 20676)
events.description: South Korea's Framework Act on the Development of Artificial Intelligence (Law No. 20676) is the first comprehensive AI regulatory statute in the Asia-Pacific region at time of enactment. It imposes risk-based obligations on AI operators, establishes an AI Safety Institute under MSIT authority, and mandates transparency and disclosure requirements.
events.investment: Regulatory framework (administrative fines up to KRW 30M / ~US$21,000 per violation via MSIT corrective-order enforcement); 10²⁶ FLOPs high-performance AI threshold (Enforcement Decree).

events.name: UK Compute Roadmap (CP 1352)
events.description: The UK government published the Compute Roadmap (CP 1352), a national AI compute strategy committing up to £2 billion to expand sovereign compute capacity from 2026–27 to 2029–30. Ten actions cover AI Research Resource expansion targeting a twentyfold increase by 2030, a new Edinburgh national supercomputer service, AI Growth Zone coordination, and international compute partnerships including EuroHPC membership.
events.investment: Up to £2B (2026–30); £1B+ AIRR expansion; £750M Edinburgh supercomputer; 6GW data centre capacity target by 2030

checkpoints.aiDetail: Option A (100% AI compute content — £2B commitment, exceeds $100M threshold)

events.name: NVIDIA AI Accelerator Series
events.scope: United States (global AI hardware supply chain)
events.description: NVIDIA's data-centre accelerator line, spanning the Hopper and Blackwell generations, built around the Transformer Engine for large language model workloads and the CUDA software ecosystem. Deployed by every major hyperscaler, the line is the dominant global platform for AI training and inference.
events.investment: FY2025 Data Centre segment revenue ~US$115.2B (up 142%); an estimated 70–95% share of the AI accelerator market

events.name: AMD Instinct Series
events.scope: United States (global AI hardware supply chain)
events.description: AMD's Instinct accelerator line, built on the CDNA architecture and paired with the open-source ROCm software stack as the principal alternative to NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem. The MI300X generation carries 192GB of HBM3 memory for large-model training and inference.
events.investment: FY2024 Data Centre segment revenue US$12.6B (up 94%), with Instinct GPU sales a named growth driver

events.name: Google TPU Series
events.description: Google's Tensor Processing Unit line, the custom AI accelerator on which Google trains and serves its own Gemini frontier models. Unlike merchant silicon, TPUs are not sold as discrete hardware; they are consumed exclusively as Google Cloud compute services, spanning the v5e and v5p generations.
events.investment: Google Cloud segment revenue ~US$43.1B FY2024; TPU fleet committed to Cloud and internal Gemini training not separately disclosed

events.name: Amazon Trainium Series
events.description: Amazon's Trainium line, AWS's custom AI training silicon offered only as rentable cloud capacity rather than sold as hardware, positioned as a cost-efficient alternative to GPU-based training. Trainium2 became available through EC2 Trn2 instances and anchors Anthropic's Project Rainier training cluster.
events.investment: AWS segment revenue ~US$107.6B FY2024 (Trainium not separately disclosed); Project Rainier deployment of hundreds of thousands of Trainium2 chips for Anthropic
